question: Snigur vs. Kawa: Set 1 Games O/U 10.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Daria Snigur and Katarzyna Kawa in the Australian Open, Qualification WTA, scheduled for January 12 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 11. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam results.

This market refers on the tennis match between Daria Snigur and Katarzyna Kawa in the Australian Open, Qualification WTA, scheduled for January 12 at 6:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Kawa' if Katarzyna Kawa advances against Daria Snigur.

This market refers to the tennis match between Alice Rame and Jazmin Ortenzi in the Australian Open, Qualification WTA, scheduled for January 12 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 10. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam results.
groupItemTitle: Australian Open Women's: Alice Rame vs Jazmin Ortenzi Set 1 O/U 9.5

events.title: Australian Open Women's: Alice Rame vs Jazmin Ortenzi
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Alice Rame and Jazmin Ortenzi in the Australian Open, Qualification WTA, scheduled for January 12 at 12:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Rame' if Alice Rame advances against Jazmin Ortenzi.

This market will resolve to 'Ortenzi' if Jazmin Ortenzi advances against Alice Rame.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market refers on the tennis match between Tristan Boyer and Alex Barrena in the Australian Open, Qualification ATP, scheduled for January 11 at 10:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Boyer' if Tristan Boyer advances against Alex Barrena.

This market will resolve to 'Barrena' if Alex Barrena advances against Tristan Boyer.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Boyer vs. Barrena: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
description:

Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.

Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam results.

This market refers on the tennis match between Olivia Gadecki and Arantxa Rus in the Australian Open, Qualification WTA, scheduled for January 11 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Gadecki' if Olivia Gadecki advances against Arantxa Rus.

This market will resolve to 'Rus' if Arantxa Rus advances against Olivia Gadecki.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market refers on the tennis match between Arthur Fery and Edward Winter in the Australian Open, Qualification ATP, scheduled for January 11 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Fery' if Arthur Fery advances against Edward Winter.

This market will resolve to 'Winter' if Edward Winter advances against Arthur Fery.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Set 1 Winner: Fery vs Winter
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Arthur Fery and Edward Winter in the Australian Open, Qualification ATP, scheduled for January 12 2026.

If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam match statistics.
